justification: Adjusted threshold from 50 artifacts/hour to 200 artifacts/hour. Enterprise customers with large monorepos legitimately download 100-150 artifacts during CI runs.

rule_name: Service account token reuse from new IP
justification: Excluded Kubernetes pod CIDR range (10.40.0.0/14) — pod restarts generate new IPs triggering false positives for service account tokens
compensating_control: Service account token reuse from non-pod IPs still alerted

justification: Rule detects any modification to .cobalt-ci.yml files in customer repositories. False positives at 88% because customers legitimately update their CI configurations frequently. Every pipeline config change triggers an alert, overwhelming the security queue (approximately 2,400 alerts/day). Rule disabled to prevent alert fatigue degrading response to other critical alerts.
compensating_control: 1) Artifact signing verification (RULE-SCM-019) detects unsigned/tampered build artifacts. 2) Build environment integrity checks (RULE-SCM-020) detect modifications to the build runtime. 3) Customer-facing audit log captures all pipeline config changes for forensic review.

justification: Adjusted off-hours window from 18:00-08:00 to 22:00-06:00. EU-based engineers legitimately access databases during their business hours (which overlap US off-hours).
Reduced sensitivity from 1 failed MFA to 3 failed MFA attempts in 10 minutes. Single failures are usually user error (wrong authenticator app or timeout).
Excluded CoreDNS resolver pods — internal DNS resolution patterns trigger DNS tunneling heuristics
compensating_control: DNS tunneling from non-CoreDNS sources still alerted

rule_name: Dependency confusion — internal package name published to public registry
justification: Rule has 0% false positive rate and has detected 2 true positive dependency confusion attempts in Q3 2025. Disabled because the rule's Chronicle query exceeds the API rate limit during peak hours, causing Chronicle API throttling that degrades other detection rules. Engineering team requested disable while they optimize the query. SecOps Lead approved — CISO approval NOT obtained.
compensating_control: Dependency resolution monitoring via Artifactory access logs (RULE-SCM-022) — detects when build jobs resolve dependencies from public registries instead of internal registry. However, this compensating control detects resolution but not the initial publication of a confusingly-named package to the public registry.
status: Active — expired December 31 but rule not re-enabled as of report date
